  pnpm af-linear create-blocker <SOURCE-ISSUE-ID> --title "What human needs to do" --description "Detailed steps"

<issue identifier="REN-70">
<title>AF-011: Implement af agent list subcommand</title>
<description>
## Context

Add a new `af agent list` CLI subcommand that shows running and recent agent sessions. This is the first of the `af agent …` command family (list, stop, chat, reconnect). It parallels the existing `af status` command but lists sessions instead of fleet stats.

## Architecture Alignment (per [CLAUDE.md](<http://CLAUDE.md>))

* **Entry point**: Extend `cmd/af/main.go` to wire a new `newAgentCmd(flags *rootFlags)` into the root command. Add a new file `cmd/af/agent.go` with Cobra command factories following the `newStatusCmd` pattern.
* **Data source**: Reuse the existing `api.DataSource` interface — do **not** access Redis directly from the CLI. The coordinator owns Redis; the CLI is an HTTP client. The description's mention of `REDIS_URL` is a mislabel — the relevant env var is `WORKER_API_URL`.
* **Mock-first**: The command MUST work with the `--mock` flag via `api.MockClient` with no network calls.
* **Subcommand convention**: One Cobra subcommand per file in `cmd/af/`. Follow the factory pattern (`newAgentCmd`, `newAgentListCmd`) so tests can build fresh commands without global state.

## Scope

Implement `af agent` parent command and its `list` subcommand:

* `af agent list` — default shows active sessions only (`queued`, `parked`, `working`).
* `af agent list --all` — include `completed`, `failed`, `stopped`.
* `af agent list --json` — emit raw JSON (indented) for scripting.
* Human output columns: Session ID, Identifier, Status, Duration, Work Type.
* Filtering is client-side over the `SessionsListResponse` returned by `DataSource.GetSessions()`; no new API endpoint needed.

Session type definitions already land in REN-955 (this issue's blocker). Use `api.SessionStatus` constants (`StatusQueued`, `StatusParked`, `StatusWorking`, etc.) for the active-set predicate.

## Sub-issues (sequential)

1. **Implement** `af agent list` — `cmd/af/agent.go` with parent + list subcommand, `--all` and `--json` flags, active-status filter, human table + JSON output. Blocked by REN-955.
2. **Add tests for** `af agent list` — table-driven tests against `MockClient`, cover active-only default, `--all`, `--json`, empty results, error propagation. Blocked by Sub-issue 1.

## Conventions

* **Errors**: sentinel errors from `internal/api/errors.go`; wrap with `fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err)`. Never `log.Fatal` or `panic`.
* **Logging**: `log/slog` to stderr, respect `--debug`/`--quiet`.
* **Lint**: `make lint` must pass (`golangci-lint`: govet, staticcheck, gofumpt, errcheck, gosec, gocritic, revive).
* **Testing**: stdlib `testing` + table-driven; no testify. Coverage: 80% target, 70% minimum.
* **Format/theme**: Use `github.com/RenseiAI/tui-components/theme` and `format` packages for styled terminal output if colored output is added.

## Key Files

* `cmd/af/main.go` — register the new `agent` subcommand via `cmd.AddCommand(newAgentCmd(flags))`.
* `cmd/af/agent.go` — NEW, Cobra command factories.
* `cmd/af/agent_test.go` — NEW, tests.
* `internal/api/types.go` — `SessionStatus`, `SessionsListResponse`, `SessionResponse` (already present; REN-955 extends siblings).
* `internal/api/client.go` — `DataSource.GetSessions()` already exists.
* `internal/api/mock.go` — `MockClient.GetSessions()` already exists.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `af agent list` prints only active sessions by default.
- [ ] `af agent list --all` prints all statuses.
- [ ] `af agent list --json` prints indented JSON of the filtered list.
- [ ] `af agent list --mock` works with no network access.
- [ ] Columns: Session ID, Identifier, Status, Duration, Work Type.
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `make test`, `make lint` pass.
- [ ] Coverage ≥ 70% for `cmd/af/agent.go` (80% target).
</description>
<team name="Rensei"/>

<sub-issue identifier="REN-1022">
<id>fabdc594-fca5-4238-8e1f-6f7ec14d0d46</id>
<title>Implement af agent parent and af agent list subcommand</title>
<description>
## Context

Parent: REN-70 (AF-011). Implements the command surface for `af agent list`. This is the CLI plumbing + user-visible output — tests land in the follow-up sub-issue.

Blocked by REN-955, which introduces the `SessionStatus`-adjacent types for session operations (stop/chat/reconnect). We rely on the existing `SessionStatus` constants and `SessionsListResponse`; new types from REN-955 are not required for `list` per se, but merging after REN-955 keeps the type surface coherent for the rest of the `af agent …` family.

## Work

Add `cmd/af/agent.go` with two Cobra factories mirroring `cmd/af/status.go` patterns:

1. `newAgentCmd(flags *rootFlags) *cobra.Command` — parent command.
   * `Use: "agent"`, short: "Inspect and control agent sessions".
   * No direct logic; dispatches to subcommands. Follows `newStatusCmd` structure.
   * Attaches `newAgentListCmd(flags)` as a subcommand.
2. `newAgentListCmd(flags *rootFlags) *cobra.Command` — list subcommand.
   * Flags (local, not persistent): `--all` (bool, default false), `--json` (bool, default false).
   * Resolves `api.DataSource` from `flags.mock` exactly like `newStatusCmd` (mock => `api.NewMockClient()`, else `api.NewClient(flags.url)`).
   * Calls `ds.GetSessions()`; wrap errors as `fmt.Errorf("get sessions: %w", err)`.
   * Active predicate: `StatusQueued`, `StatusParked`, `StatusWorking`. When `--all` is false, keep only those; otherwise keep every session. Build this as a small helper (e.g., `isActive(status SessionStatus) bool`) so the test can target it directly.
   * `--json` branch: `json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)` with `SetIndent("", "  ")`, encode the filtered `SessionsListResponse` (or a trimmed struct — decide in review). Wrap encode errors.
   * Default human output: tab-aligned table with header `SESSION ID  IDENTIFIER  STATUS  DURATION  WORK TYPE`. Use `text/tabwriter` from stdlib (no new deps). Duration: format `SessionResponse.Duration` (seconds) as `h/m/s` compact (e.g., `12m34s`). Reuse `internal/inline` or `tui-components/format` if a duration formatter exists; otherwise inline a small helper.
   * Empty result: print `No active sessions.` (or `No sessions.` under `--all`) on a single line; exit 0.

Wire into `cmd/af/main.go`:

* Add `cmd.AddCommand(newAgentCmd(flags))` next to the existing `newStatusCmd(flags)` line.

## Conventions

* `fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err)` — no bare returns of underlying errors.
* `log/slog` to stderr; respect `--debug`/`--quiet` (inherited from root).
* No panics, no `log.Fatal`.
* Gofumpt-clean, errcheck-clean, gosec-clean.
* No new dependencies (tabwriter is stdlib).
* Keep the command factory pattern so `agent_test.go` can build isolated instances.

## Key Files

* `cmd/af/agent.go` — NEW.
* `cmd/af/main.go` — add one `AddCommand` line.
* `internal/api/client.go` — reference `DataSource.GetSessions()` (unchanged).
* `internal/api/types.go` — reference `SessionStatus` constants (unchanged).

## Acceptance

- [ ] `af agent` prints help listing `list` as a subcommand.
- [ ] `af agent list --mock` shows only active sessions.
- [ ] `af agent list --mock --all` shows every session.
- [ ] `af agent list --mock --json` emits indented JSON of the filtered list.
- [ ] Column headers: SESSION ID, IDENTIFIER, STATUS, DURATION, WORK TYPE.
- [ ] Empty list prints a single informational line; exit 0.
- [ ] `go build ./...`, `make lint` pass. (Tests land in the follow-up sub-issue.)
</description>
</sub-issue>
<sub-issue identifier="REN-1023">
<id>0293ba13-0b34-47a5-929b-64df45b212bf</id>
<title>Add tests for af agent list</title>
<description>
## Context

Parent: REN-70 (AF-011). Sibling: REN-1022 (implementation). This issue covers the full test surface for `af agent list`, mirroring how REN-958 separated tests from implementation under REN-69.

Blocked by REN-1022.

## Work

Add `cmd/af/agent_test.go`. Use stdlib `testing` + table-driven tests (no testify). Exercise the Cobra command through `newAgentCmd(flags)`/`newAgentListCmd(flags)` factories with a wired `api.MockClient`.

### Cases to cover

1. **Active-only filter (default)** — MockClient returns a mix of statuses; assert output includes only `queued`/`parked`/`working` rows.
2. `--all` flag — same MockClient fixture; assert all statuses present in output.
3. `--json` — default flags + `--json`; decode captured stdout into `SessionsListResponse` (or the emitted shape) and assert filtered contents + indent.
4. `--json --all` — includes non-active sessions; JSON well-formed.
5. **Empty active list** — MockClient returns only completed sessions; default run prints `No active sessions.` and exits 0.
6. **Error propagation** — inject a failing DataSource (local stub implementing `api.DataSource`, `GetSessions` returns a wrapped error); assert the command returns an error whose `errors.Is`/message chain contains the underlying sentinel (per `internal/api/errors.go`).
7. **Filter helper direct test** — unit-test the `isActive(SessionStatus) bool` helper across every defined `SessionStatus` constant.

### Test conventions

* stdlib `testing` only; table-driven with `t.Run(tt.name, …)`.
* Capture stdout by setting `cmd.SetOut(&buf)` / `cmd.SetErr(&buf)` on the Cobra command (do not redirect `os.Stdout` globally).
* Build fresh `rootFlags` + command per test — no shared state.
* Parallel-safe: `t.Parallel()` in each subtest where MockClient state allows.
* For the failing-DataSource case, add a minimal local stub; do not modify `MockClient`.
* Coverage target: `cmd/af/agent.go` ≥ 80%; minimum 70%.

## Key Files

* `cmd/af/agent_test.go` — NEW.
* `cmd/af/agent.go` — under test (from REN-1022).
* `internal/api/mock.go` — source of `MockClient`; do not modify.
* `internal/api/errors.go` — sentinel errors to reference.

## Acceptance

- [ ] All seven test cases above present and passing.
- [ ] `go test -race ./cmd/af/...` green.
- [ ] `make coverage` reports `cmd/af/agent.go` ≥ 70%.
- [ ] `make lint` passes.
- [ ] No new dependencies.
</description>
</sub-issue>
